events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

Essentrics: Mobility, Strength, Flexibility, Range of Motion
events.description: Essentrics is a full body workout that unlocks and decompresses joints by stretching and strengthening all 650 muscles without impact to the body. The workout incorporates flowing movements designed to increase circulation with the benefits of elongating and lubricating connective tissues around muscles and joints. This unique, re-aligning, and rebalancing approach to body maintenance encompasses trademark sequences that lead to improved wellness, increased energy, and pain reduction. Every time you come to an Essentrics class, you will enjoy fresh music playlists and new exercise sequences. There’s no equipment needed for Essentrics, all that is required is a mat. Instructor: Junko Hammond Note: Drop-in passes are available for this class. Please contact our front desk for more information. Price: $11 per class

events.title: Drop in Kindergym
events.description:
**Construction in the downstairs area of QVCC continues. As a result we will be operating playgroups at a limited capacity with access to only one side of our basement. Lots of play could occur outside on our playground so come prepared for the weather. We will still offer snack as per usual and fun to be had. We apologize for any inconvenience, if you have any further questions please reach out to earlyyears@quadravillagecc.com**

Drop-in Program. For parents/caregivers and their children (1-5yrs.) to enjoy our toys, tricycles, cars and trucks, teeter-totters and other large motor development toys. Adults are welcome to relax and get to know other neighbourhood families and QVCC facilitators while their children play. Snack provided. Program takes place in the QVCC Neighbourhood Gym at 950 Kings Rd. (the entrance is at the back ofthe building through the parking lot).
There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations. Calendar: Child & Family

events.title: All Levels Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! All skill levels welcome. Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles are available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Yoga - Hatha Flow*
events.description: Traditional Hatha postures are sequenced to encourage fluid movement between postures. This fluid movement, partnered with breath awareness, helps create a strong connection between mind, body and spirit.  This class is suitable for all-levels and blends stretching, strengthening, balancing, and relaxation. Please bring your own mat. *Specialty Yoga Wellness Drop-in $18/class.

events.title: Games Night and Swim + Dinner 9-17yrs - Gordon Head
events.description: Dive into fun! Our Games Night and Swim program kicks off with a delicious dinner followed by staff led group activities, games, and recreational swimming. From classic board and card games to friendly matches of playing pool, there’s something for everyone. Youth will get to participate in group games that encourage teamwork, movement, and laughter. The day wraps up with supervised swim time, where participants can enjoy the pool in a laid-back, social setting. Whether your child is outgoing or prefers a quieter space to hang out, our Games Night and Swim program offers the perfect balance of activity, relaxation, and connection.

events.title: Wheelchair Tennis | Drop-In
events.description:
Learn to play or advance your skills in wheelchair tennis. It’s the same sport you know and love – just permitting two bounces instead of one! 

Registered drop-in for one session. Weekly, recurring program also available. Sign up for that may be found under the Programs section of Panorama's website.

Program geared towards players of beginner to low intermediate skill level. Sessions will include practice and drills intended to develop key techniques and tactics, along with racquet and on-court movement skills, plus some friendly match play for the opportunity to put learnings into action. 

******

If you would like to discuss whether or not this program could be a good fit for you, or borrow a wheelchair, please contact Panorama’s Racquet Sports Coordinator prior to program start - (250) 656-7271 or info@panoramarec.bc.ca. 

******

We would like to acknowledge Tennis Canada, the Government of Canada and BC Wheelchair Sports Association for, via the First Serve Wheelchair Tennis Funding Program and Canadian Sport for All Initiative, subsidizing this programming! 

Nous tenons à remercier Tennis Canada, le Gouvernement du Canada et la BC Wheelchair Sports Association qui, par l’entremise du programme de financement First Serve Wheelchair Tennis et de l’Initiative canadienne pour le sport communautaire, ont permis de subventionner cette programmation !

******

Withdrawal for full refund permitted with at least 24hrs' notice. Withdrawal may be completed via your online account or Reception.

events.title: Everyone Welcome and Programs
events.description: Aquatic Fitness, Swimming Lessons and/or clubs take up dedicated space in the Leisure and/or Lap Pool. (See waterfit schedule.) All other pool space including Lifestyle Pool, Tots Pool Hot Tub, Sauna, Steam Room, majority of Lap Pool open for the public to enjoy. Children under 7 must be within arms reach of a responsible adult at all times. One lane available for lap swimming.

events.title: All Levels Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! All skill levels welcome. Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles are available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Drop-In Playgroup with Messy Hands activity
events.facility_name: 901 Kings Road. Around the back of the building
events.description:
**Construction in the downstairs area of QVCC continues. As a result we will be operating playgroups at a limited capacity with access to only one side of our basement. Lots of play could occur outside on our playground so come prepared for the weather. We will still offer snack as per usual and fun to be had. We apologize for any inconvenience, if you have any further questions please reach out to earlyyears@quadravillagecc.com**

Children 1-5yrs. and their adults can play and learn in a relaxed social setting with arts and crafts, toys, books, puzzles.  For children 2years+,  there is the option of a Messy Hands activities (paints, play dough, water table etc.). Please note that children must be past the stage of putting things into their mouths to participate in the Messy Hands activities. Parents are also expected to closely supervise their children.

Snack provided There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  

Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations.  
If you have questions, please email earlyyears@quadravillagecc.com 
or call 250 388-7696 ext. 226 For: Parents/Caregivers and their children ages 0-5yrs Calendar: Child & Family

events.title: Pickleball Reserved Drop-in
events.description:
Pickleball is a racquet sport that combines elements of badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Players use solid paddles to hit a perforated polymer ball, similar to a wiffle ball, over a net. The sport shares features of other racquet sports, the dimensions and layout of a badminton court, and a net and rules similar to tennis, with a few modifications.
Players can reserve for their desired session up to 7 days in advance. Register via phone/in person Sun-Sat 5:30am-10:30pm or online 24 hours a day, register at your convenience!

events.title: Open Court - Lacrosse & Ball Hockey | Sport Box
events.description:
Court reserved for walk-up lacrosse, ball hockey and roller hockey use! Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. Sharing of surface between sport types permitted - conditional upon it being approached in a safe, respectful and contained way. 

******

Wearing of appropriate safety gear strongly recommended - including helmets and gloves. Closed-toe, athletic shoes with non-marking soles required. Nets provided (will be on or around exterior perimetre of surface). Bring your own sticks, balls and other required equipment. 

******

Users of all ages welcome! Users under the age of 12 must be accompanied by a responsible adult.

******

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and to take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Intermediate (3.0-3.5) Pickleball |Reservable
events.description:
Join us for Intermediate Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! Intermediate, 3.0-3.5 skill levels welcome (refer to Pickleball Canada’s self-rating guide). Bring your own paddle and ball.

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.description: Come and challenge yourself in this 50+ minute cycling class, including: intervals, drills, sprints and climbs both in and out of the saddle. Motivational music will keep you energized on the Keiser spin bike during the entire class. Bring your own yoga mat to class.

events.title: PKS HIIT and Stretch *** Reserved Drop In
events.description: Participate in short periods of high intensity exercise followed by less intense recovery periods. The bursts of intensity will burn fat and increase metabolism long after the workout. You set your own level of intensity. Class ends with a 10 min relaxing stretch.

events.title: SCP Cardio Fit** - Reserved Drop In
events.description: Come ready to work and burn calories in this high energy, high cardio and high impact fitness class that uses Tabata, HIIT, intervals and aerobic patterns to burn calories.  Modifications can be shown.

events.title: FitTok - Youth Fitness, Health and Wellness Group 10-17yrs
events.description: FitTok - Where fun meets fitness and mental strength! This high-energy youth program is designed to help youth build strong bodies and resilient minds through a mix of exciting physical challenges and chill, brain-boosting activities. From obstacle courses and team relays to yoga flows and TikTok like dances—every week brings a new way to get moving, build strength, and boost energy. After the action, we cool down with guided mindfulness exercises, creative journaling, confidence-building discussions, and breathing techniques to easy every day stressors. Some weeks youth will have the opportunity to make healthy snack/smoothie options in our kitchen.

events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Deep Fit
events.description:
Moderate intensity, no impact (comfort in deep water required).
Using a belt or a noodle, this class is a great way for those with injuries or other limitations to still experience a good cardiovascular workout. This class will include strength and abdominal exercises to ensure a full body workout with no strain on bones or joints. 
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

events.title: All Levels Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! All skill levels welcome. Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles are available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Kids Night Out
events.description: Calling all parents and guardians of children ages 4 - 10. Are you keen to have a date night, run errands or be in your home kid free for a few hours on a Friday or Saturday night? The SCP youth centre is the place to be. Register your child for a night of fun, games, movies, toys, crafts and so much more with our staff team. Children ages 8+ will have the opportunity to go swimming during this program with a staff member. Cheese pizza, cucumbers and apples will be served for diner and is included with each registration fee. Drop between 4:30 and 5:00Pick up between 8:30 and 9:30All children attending this program must be able to use the bathroom independently and should be comfortable in a setting away from a parental figure for an extended period of time.

events.title: Friday Night Social 10-17yrs - Pickleball and Picnic Night!
events.description: Looking for a fun way to kick off your weekend? Swing by our supervised Friday Night Social Drop-In at Gordon Head Recreation Centre—a free, casual hangout where you can chill with friends, meet new people, and dive into a variety of awesome activities. Each week has different staff-led activities. Whether you’re into pool, ping pong, or testing your skills at arcade basketball, we’ve got you covered. Game lovers can compete on the PS5 gaming system, or challenge our staff to board games and card games for some friendly competition. Feeling creative? Try your hand at bracelet making or other fun arts and crafts projects. Plus, we’ll fuel the night with a tasty dinner, so you can relax, hang out, and enjoy great food while making memories.Closed on stat holidays and Pro D Days. Must be at least 10 years old and in grade 5 to attend this program.

events.title: TnT: Techniques & Tactics | Tennis
events.description:
For advanced players of a self-assessed 4.0+ skill level, as per Tennis Canada’s Self-Rating Guide, who are looking to advance their skills through drills that work to develop techniques and tactics in a game-based setting. 

Reserve your spot online, via the Activity Schedules > Sports section of our website, for this popular weekly offering at 7:30am Friday for the following week’s session!

******

As a pre-requisite, registrants should have a good understanding of the game, be able to rally 10 balls in a row from the baseline with top spin (with both forehands and backhands), and be able to serve with spin and volley deep off low balls. 

REGISTRATION NOTES: 
- Registration opens at 7:30am Friday for the following week's session. Registration closes 48 hours prior to each session. 
- As a registered drop-in class, at least 24hrs' notice is required for withdrawal and full refund. Withdrawal may be completed online via your client account - or with assistance of Panorama Reception. info@panoramarec.bc.ca

events.title: $2 Table Tennis | Reservable
events.description:
$2 table tennis! All skill levels welcome. Reserve your spot in advance or drop-in (space permitting).

Time coincides with Greater Victoria Table Tennis Club League play. One table will be available for community use (others will be for league use). Timeslots on the hour. Please check-in with Reception upon arrival. Continued play's welcome - space permitting. Please check with Reception at the start of the next session. 

Paddles and balls provided (from Reception) - or bring your own. Shoes must have non-marking soles.

Four players max per timeslot (more permitted, along with those under 12 yrs, if of same group). 

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Fun Swim (S)

(S) indicates shared space with programs or rental groups.

events.title: $2 Table Tennis | Reservable
events.description:
$2 table tennis! All skill levels welcome. Reserve your spot in advance or drop-in (space permitting).

Time coincides with Greater Victoria Table Tennis Club League play. One table will be available for community use (others will be for league use). Timeslots on the hour. Please check-in with Reception upon arrival. Continued play's welcome - space permitting. Please check with Reception at the start of the next session. 

Paddles and balls provided (from Reception) - or bring your own. Shoes must have non-marking soles.

Four players max per timeslot (more permitted, along with those under 12 yrs, if of same group). 

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: All Levels Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! All skill levels welcome. Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles are available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Open Court - Convertible Net High | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to badminton/volleyball height or pickleball height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. 

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting.

events.description: Enjoy shooting on the net without goalies. Helmet, gloves, stick and skates mandatory. You must reserve your spot to participate. There are no goalies, scrimmages, drills or passing down the ice. Keep the front and behind the net clear at all times. Always close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ Goalie
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Gold - Lesson Area 2
events.description: Enjoy practicing your goaltending in the net during a Stick and Puck session. Full gear required. Maximum 2 goalies can register. You must reserve your spot to participate. Any goalie not registered as a goalie will be asked to remain a skater. Only one net is used for goalies, the other net will remain open. Goalies must be respectful and take turns being in net. Close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation. Please refer to the guidelines on our website for further details

events.title: Pickleball Reserved Drop-in
events.description:
Pickleball is a racquet sport that combines elements of badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Players use solid paddles to hit a perforated polymer ball, similar to a wiffle ball, over a net. The sport shares features of other racquet sports, the dimensions and layout of a badminton court, and a net and rules similar to tennis, with a few modifications.
Players can reserve for their desired session up to 7 days in advance. Register via phone/in person Sun-Sat 5:30am-10:30pm or online 24 hours a day, register at your convenience!

events.title: Pickleball at the Sport Box | All Levels | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at the Sport Box at Centennial Park. $5 per person or a "swipe" of your Active Pass! All skill levels welcome. Please bring your own paddle and ball (a couple will be available for borrow). Player assistance with net set up and take down's appreciated.

Reservation required in advance (due to the off-site nature of sessions). Please check-in with session facilitator upon arrival. If reservation was completed on the day of the session, please be prepared to show proof of payment (receipt) to the facilitator. Refund/cancellation information below.

******

Sport Box Facility:  Covered, open-air multi-sport box with acrylic-coated playing surface (like an outdoor tennis court). Lines and portable nets available for four pickleball courts (regulation-sized). Washrooms available at the adjacent Fieldhouse. Zero threshold entrance to playing surface available on parking lot side (left door). Only certified and assistance animals permitted on the surface. Please not food or drink, other than water, on the surface. Water fountain available seasonally adjacent to the baseball diamond. 

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Play Beyond Expectations
events.description:
Play Beyond Expectations provides a fun and relaxed opportunity to learn different sports and social skills in an environment that welcomes young adults of all levels of functioning ability.  Previous experience with sports is not required. This program focusses on enjoyable skill games based on floor hockey, basketball, pickleball and group games tailored to each person’s abilities.
Players and volunteers all enjoy the accepting and encouraging atmosphere and building on their often unexpected successes.  The lead volunteers are themselves people who have overcome physical challenges to find success in sports and in life.  The improved physical activity, coordination, and cooperation skills gained in the gym transfer to greater success in other activities at home and in the community.  Each player is recognized for their best moment in every session.  Each individual success is an opportunity for a group celebration.  Come join us for weekly drop in!

events.title: Open Court - Lacrosse & Ball Hockey | Sport Box (*Busy Parking Anticipated)
events.description:
Court reserved for walk-up lacrosse, ball hockey and roller hockey use! Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. Sharing of surface between sport types permitted - conditional upon it being approached in a safe, respectful and contained way. 

******

Wearing of appropriate safety gear strongly recommended - including helmets and gloves. Closed-toe, athletic shoes with non-marking soles required. Nets provided (will be on or around exterior perimetre of surface). Bring your own sticks, balls and other required equipment. 

******

Users of all ages welcome! Guardian or adult supervision expected for those 10 years and younger.

******

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and to take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Wheelchair Tennis | Drop-In
events.description:
Learn to play or advance your skills in wheelchair tennis. It’s the same sport you know and love – just permitting two bounces instead of one! 

Registered drop-in for one session. Weekly, recurring program also available. Sign up for that may be found under the Programs section of Panorama's website.

Program geared towards players of beginner to low intermediate skill level. Sessions will include practice and drills intended to develop key techniques and tactics, along with racquet and on-court movement skills, plus some friendly match play for the opportunity to put learnings into action. 

******

If you would like to discuss whether or not this program could be a good fit for you, or borrow a wheelchair, please contact Panorama’s Racquet Sports Coordinator prior to program start - (250) 656-7271 or info@panoramarec.bc.ca. 

******

We would like to acknowledge Tennis Canada, the Government of Canada and BC Wheelchair Sports Association for, via the First Serve Wheelchair Tennis Funding Program and Canadian Sport for All Initiative, subsidizing this programming! 

Nous tenons à remercier Tennis Canada, le Gouvernement du Canada et la BC Wheelchair Sports Association qui, par l’entremise du programme de financement First Serve Wheelchair Tennis et de l’Initiative canadienne pour le sport communautaire, ont permis de subventionner cette programmation !

******

Withdrawal for full refund permitted with at least 24hrs' notice. Withdrawal may be completed via your online account or Reception.

events.description:
Pickleball is a racquet sport that combines elements of badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Players use solid paddles to hit a perforated polymer ball, similar to a wiffle ball, over a net. The sport shares features of other racquet sports, the dimensions and layout of a badminton court, and a net and rules similar to tennis, with a few modifications.
Players can reserve for their desired session up to 7 days in advance. Register via phone/in person Sun-Sat 5:30am-10:30pm or online 24 hours a day, register at your convenience!

events.description: Looking for a fun way to kick off your weekend? Swing by our supervised Friday Night Social Drop-In at Gordon Head Recreation Centre—a free, casual hangout where you can chill with friends, meet new people, and dive into a variety of awesome activities. Each week has different staff-led activities. Whether you’re into pool, ping pong, or testing your skills at arcade basketball, we’ve got you covered. Game lovers can compete on the PS5 gaming system, or challenge our staff to board games and card games for some friendly competition. Feeling creative? Try your hand at bracelet making or other fun arts and crafts projects. Plus, we’ll fuel the night with a tasty dinner, so you can relax, hang out, and enjoy great food while making memories.Closed on stat holidays and Pro D Days. Must be at least 10 years old and in grade 5 to attend this program.

Are you keen to have a date night, run errands or be in your home kid free for a few hours on a Friday or Saturday night? The SCP youth centre is the place to be. Register your child for a night of fun, games, movies, toys, crafts and so much more with our staff team. Children ages 8+ will have the opportunity to go swimming during this program with a staff member. Cheese pizza, cucumbers and apples will be served for diner and is included with each registration fee. Drop between 4:30 and 5:00Pick up between 8:30 and 9:30All children attending this program must be able to use the bathroom independently and should be comfortable in a setting away from a parental figure for an extended period of time.

Must be 16 or older, please bring your own paddle.

events.title: Indoor Soccer 16+
events.description:
Recreational indoor soccer for ages 16 + .
Players can reserve for their desired session up to 7 days in advance. Register via phone/in person Sun-Sat 5:30am-10:30pm or online 24 hours a day, register at your convenience! Note: Payment is required at time of reservation. If you have a valid pass (membership), the free “included with pass” payment option will populate; please register yourself at no charge.  If the free “included with pass” payment option does not populate, this means that you do not have a valid pass. Please contact reception in person or by phone at 250.412.8500 to renew your pass.

events.title: Drop in Kindergym
events.description:
Drop-in Program. For parents/caregivers and their children (1-5yrs.) to enjoy our toys, tricycles, cars and trucks, teeter-totters and other large motor development toys. Adults are welcome to relax and get to know other neighbourhood families and QVCC facilitators while their children play. Snack provided. Program takes place in the QVCC Neighbourhood Gym at 950 Kings Rd. (the entrance is at the back ofthe building through the parking lot).
There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations. Calendar: Child & Family

events.title: Tiny Tykes Playgroup (0-5 yrs) - Thurs | Spring (OSC Room)
Drop-In Playgroup with Messy Hands activity
events.facility_name: 901 Kings Road. Around the back of the building
events.description:
Children 1-5yrs. and their adults can play and learn in a relaxed social setting with arts and crafts, toys, books, puzzles.  For children 2years+,  there is the option of a Messy Hands activities (paints, play dough, water table etc.). Please note that children must be past the stage of putting things into their mouths to participate in the Messy Hands activities. Parents are also expected to closely supervise their children.

Snack provided There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  

Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations.  
If you have questions, please email earlyyears@quadravillagecc.com 
or call 250 388-7696 ext. 226 For: Parents/Caregivers and their children ages 0-5yrs Calendar: Child & Family

events.title: XL Yoga
events.price: $96 for the whole session or $15 drop-in
events.description: Yoga for those living in large bodies taught by a person living in a large body. The benefits of yoga – increased balance, flexibility, stamina, and relaxation – are wonderful for everybody. We’ll make the poses work for everyone using modifications and props (bolsters, blankets, and straps). Our classes are fun and light-hearted. Instructor: Jill Moran Note: Fees will be prorated for late registrations. Price: $96 for the whole session or $15 drop-in

events.title: Tiny Tykes Playgroup (0-5 yrs) - Thurs | Spring (OSC Room)
Drop-In Playgroup with Messy Hands activity
events.facility_name: 901 Kings Road. Around the back of the building
events.description:
Children 1-5yrs. and their adults can play and learn in a relaxed social setting with arts and crafts, toys, books, puzzles.  For children 2years+,  there is the option of a Messy Hands activities (paints, play dough, water table etc.). Please note that children must be past the stage of putting things into their mouths to participate in the Messy Hands activities. Parents are also expected to closely supervise their children.

Snack provided There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  

Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations.  
If you have questions, please email earlyyears@quadravillagecc.com 
or call 250 388-7696 ext. 226 For: Parents/Caregivers and their children ages 0-5yrs Calendar: Child & Family

events.price: $96 for the whole session or $15 drop-in
events.description: Yoga for those living in large bodies taught by a person living in a large body. The benefits of yoga – increased balance, flexibility, stamina, and relaxation – are wonderful for everybody. We’ll make the poses work for everyone using modifications and props (bolsters, blankets, and straps). Our classes are fun and light-hearted. Instructor: Jill Moran Note: Fees will be prorated for late registrations. Price: $96 for the whole session or $15 drop-in

Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations.

Drop-In Playgroup with Messy Hands activity
events.facility_name: 901 Kings Road. Around the back of the building
events.description:
Children 1-5yrs. and their adults can play and learn in a relaxed social setting with arts and crafts, toys, books, puzzles.  For children 2years+,  there is the option of a Messy Hands activities (paints, play dough, water table etc.). Please note that children must be past the stage of putting things into their mouths to participate in the Messy Hands activities. Parents are also expected to closely supervise their children.

Snack provided There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  

Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations.  
If you have questions, please email earlyyears@quadravillagecc.com 
or call 250 388-7696 ext. 226 For: Parents/Caregivers and their children ages 0-5yrs Calendar: Child & Family

events.title: Drop-In Playgroup
events.facility_name: 901 Kings Road. Around the back of the building
events.description:
Drop-in program. Playgroup is for parents/caregivers and their children (5 and under) to play together with our puzzles, books, art, toys and more! Program is open for 2 hours and you are welcome to come and go as you like. Breakfast provided around 11-11:30am.
There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  
Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations.  
If you have questions, please email earlyyears@quadravillagecc.com For: Parents/Caregivers and their children ages 0-5yrs Calendar: Child & Family

events.description: A fun swim time for all ages! Pool toys, spray features and the waterslide are available. You mut be 48" tall to ride the waterslide. Steam room, sauna and hot tub are open.     CHILD ADMISSION POLICY:  Children under the age of 7 must be within arm’s reach of a responsible person 16 years or older at all times, including in changerooms, on the pool deck, and in the pool. A maximum of 3 children under 7 may be supervised by a responsible person.  Children 7-9 years must have an adult in the facility during their visit Spaces: 160

events.title: Fun Swim with Waterslide
events.description: A fun swim time for all ages! Pool toys, spray features and the waterslide are available. You mut be 48" tall to ride the waterslide. Steam room, sauna and hot tub are open.     CHILD ADMISSION POLICY:  Children under the age of 7 must be within arm’s reach of a responsible person 16 years or older at all times, including in changerooms, on the pool deck, and in the pool. A maximum of 3 children under 7 may be supervised by a responsible person.  Children 7-9 years must have an adult in the facility during their visit Spaces: 160
